mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: demyelination, nerve damage (demyelinating peripheral neuropathy, self-injurious behavior), vision disturbances (erythroid hyperplasia, palpebral edema), seizures
coarse facial features, bone changes, cardiac anomaly (left ventricular morphological abnormality), liver disorder (hepatic failure, hepatosplenomegaly), testicular atrophy

muscular weakness (proximal lower limb muscle weakness, pedal edema), heart complications (cardiomyopathy), gastrointestinal symptoms (diffuse abdominal pain, abdominal distension), respiratory difficulties
renal symptoms (decreased circulating IgA and IgG concentrations), angiokeratoma, hypohidrosis, gastrointestinal symptoms (diarrhea, nausea), cardiovascular manifestations (mitral valve prolapse)
cognitive decline, motor skills regression, sensory deficits (impairments to touch, position sense, vibration perception), urinary symptoms (polyuria, nocturnal enuresis), constipation
bone marrow failure leading to pancytopenia, congenital malformations (thumb or radial ray defects), skin pigmentation disorders, increased susceptibility to infection, short stature
diabetes insipidus, optic atrophy, deafness, diabetes mellitus, uremia
